name: Red Wine, Tomato & Rosemary Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Fresh rosemary, chopped OR 1 tsp Dried rosemary
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic in 2 tablespoons of water for 5 minutes.
2. Transfer the mixture to a heavy 3-quart pot.
3. Add remaining ingredients except 1/3 cup cold water and arrowroot.
4. Bring to a boil, then cover partially and simmer for 15-20 minutes until vegetables are tender.
5. Whisk together cold water and arrowroot (or cornstarch) until smooth and fully dissolved.
6. Slowly pour the mixture into the simmering sauce while stirring constantly.
7. Lower heat and continue stirring as the sauce thickens and becomes shiny, about 5 minutes.
8. If necessary, add more dissolved arrowroot or cornstarch a teaspoon at a time until the desired consistency is reached.
Variations:
- Substitute another cup of wine for one of the cups of water.
- Add 1 cup of sliced sauteed mushrooms to the sauce just before adding the arrowroot or cornstarch.
